title: cmdhelp v0.1
description: JSON Schema for output of `<cmd> help --format json` per the cmdhelp v0.1 spec. Tools advertising cmdhelp/0.1 via their capability bit emit documents matching this schema. Consumers (LLM harnesses, MCP servers, IDE plugins, wrappers) validate against this schema before parsing. See IDEA-927 for the full spec.

cmdhelp_version.description: Wire format version. MAJOR.MINOR only — never includes a PATCH component. See spec §9.

binary.description: The CLI's binary name as invoked on the command line (e.g. "pad", "git", "kubectl").

version.description: The implementation's own software version (independent from cmdhelp_version). Free-form; semver recommended.

summary.description: One-line description of the binary, suitable for an LLM context blurb.

homepage.description: Optional canonical URL for the project.

global_flags.description: Flags that apply to every command in the tree (e.g. --workspace, --format). Same shape as a per-command `flags` object.

commands.description: Map of command paths (space-joined, e.g. "item create") to their definitions.

schemas.description: Reusable JSON Schema fragments referenced by per-command stdout definitions via `json_schema_ref` (e.g. "#/schemas/Item").

context.description: Optional dynamic context spliced into help output by CLIs with session state. See spec §7.

command.description: Definition of a single command in the CLI.

description.description: Optional longer prose explanation, may include markdown.

flags.description: Map of flag names (without leading dashes) to flag definitions.

stdin.description: Whether the command accepts stdin and the expected format.

format.description: MIME-style hint such as text/markdown or application/json.

stdout.description: What the command writes to stdout on success.

text_template.description: Template string for the human-readable line; may use {placeholder} substitutions.

json_schema_ref.description: JSON Pointer (typically into the top-level `schemas` map, e.g. "#/schemas/Item") describing the JSON shape printed under --format json.

exit_codes.description: Map of exit codes (as decimal strings) to their meaning. Each entry MAY be a terse string OR a rich object — consumers MUST handle both forms (spec §5.2).

examples.description: Canonical examples — single source of truth, rendered to both --format json and --format md (spec §6).

see_also.description: Other command paths a reader might find relevant (e.g. ["item update", "item delete"]).

since.description: Implementation version in which the command first appeared (independent of cmdhelp_version).

stability.description: Stability promise. Free-form; common values: experimental, beta, stable, deprecated.

default.description: Default value if argument is omitted (when not required).

flagMap.description: Map of flag names (without leading --) to flag definitions.

default.description: Default value if flag is omitted.

repeatable.description: True if the flag may appear more than once on a single invocation.

negate_flag.description: Optional negation form for a bool flag (e.g. "--no-cache"). Only meaningful when type=bool. See spec §5.3.

allOf.description: negate_flag only applies to bool-typed flags.

typeName.description: Argument/flag type. Closed set per spec §5.1, plus x-* extension namespace for tool-specific types.

anyOf.description: Tool-specific extension type. Wrappers MAY treat unknown x-* types as `string`.

enumValues.description: Concrete list of allowed values when type=enum (or as a snapshot for dynamic enums).

enumSource.description: Reference to a command that resolves the enum dynamically at help-emission time (e.g. "dynamic:pad collection list"). The literal `enum` array, when present alongside, is a snapshot.

enumConsistency.description: If `enum` or `enum_source` is set, the type SHOULD be `enum`. Encoded as a soft constraint via if/then so this remains permissive for tools that prefer to type the slot more specifically.

exitCodeRich.description: Rich object form of an exit-code entry. The string form is also valid (see exit_codes patternProperties).

when.description: Concise description of the condition that produces this exit code.

recovery.description: Suggested user/agent action to recover from this exit code.

message_template.description: Template for the stderr message printed alongside the exit; may use {placeholder} substitutions.

example.description: A canonical example invocation. Same source for --format json and --format md (spec §6).

cmd.description: The runnable invocation. MUST resolve against the live command tree (validated in test suite per spec §6).
